cycle_label: GOV-12 — governance-thread standalone sprint · pattern-extension detector pass (D-DOC-1 doc-references-resolve + D-CFG-1 canonical-set-matches-derived) · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-12 = 12). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: GOV-12 OPEN since 2026-05-17 (same day as GOV-11 close). L01 + L02 + L03 closed. Sprint extends the GOV-11 telemetry-contract framework with pattern-generalization detectors. Manifest now tracks **12 contracts** (was 5 pre-GOV-11). **L01** fresh sweep clean (0 failed user units, all standing timers green); re-checked GOV-11's 4 carryforward drifts (3 stable + 1 worsening — git-commit `sprint_code` violations 133 → 135 in ~2h); pre-verified D-CFG-1 first-pair drift (3 inconsistencies). Operator-picked L02 bundle: D-DOC-1 + D-CFG-1-first-pair. **L02** shipped both contracts to canonical darntech (commit darntech `beedb30`); live-validated 5 pass · 7 fail · 0 warn across all 12 contracts. **D-CFG-1** (HIGH severity): surgical 3-item diff — VBC missing `LIF.FAM.CARE` + `LIF.SELF.CREATIVE` (added to taxonomy 2026-05-11 cycle-22-huey expansion, never propagated), extra `OPS.MEMORY.AUDIT` (retired same day). **D-DOC-1** (LOW severity): 1053 unresolved refs / 6025 checked / 449 active-doc files; needed 5 in-loop tuning rounds (13800 → 1053). **L03** operator-direct surgical 5-line VBC edit struck the D-CFG-1 first-pair drift in one round; `canonical-23-matches-value-bucket-config` flipped FAIL → PASS; full 12-contract run now **6 pass · 6 fail · 0 warn** (was 5 · 7 · 0). Full author-to-fix loop demonstrated end-to-end (L01 pre-verify → L02 contract surfaces → L03 operator-edit → PASS). Git-commit hook drift continues worsening (135 → 136 at L03 fire) — operator-route follow-up, not GOV-12 scope.
goals: L01 — fresh sweep + GOV-11 carryforward drift re-check + L02 pick. CLOSED — sweep clean, 3 drifts stable + 1 worsening, picked D-DOC-1 + D-CFG-1-first-pair bundle (D-CFG-1 drift pre-verified at L01: 3 inconsistencies)

L02 — author D-DOC-1 + D-CFG-1-first-pair contracts. CLOSED — both shipped to canonical darntech (commit beedb30); manifest now 12 contracts; live-validated 5 pass · 7 fail · 0 warn; D-DOC-1 needed 5 in-loop tuning rounds to bring noise floor to actionable level (13800 → 1053 unresolved); D-CFG-1 surgical with 3-item drift matching substrate pre-verification

L03 — D-CFG-1 first-pair drift fix. CLOSED — 5-line edit to `darntech/scripts/value-bucket-config.json code_to_bucket` (+LIF.FAM.CARE + LIF.SELF.CREATIVE bucket=meta · −OPS.MEMORY.AUDIT); contract flipped FAIL → PASS in one round; full 12-contract run now 6 pass · 6 fail · 0 warn (was 5 · 7 · 0); validates the D-CFG-1 detector's full author-to-fix loop end-to-end
decisions_pending_operator: L04 scope pick OR close GOV-12 — backlog items #3-8 remain (D-CFG-1 remaining 3 pairs · D-STALE trio · D-META-1 · D-DOC-1 triage · GOV-11 carryforward fixes). Highest-leverage candidates: (a) D-DOC-1 triage round 1 (operator-direct ratchet on 1060 baseline · strike `darntech-foreman/*` refs + template placeholders), (b) D-CFG-1 second-pair (Casey deployment-names ↔ darntech composables · same dispatch shape · builds toward generalized `check_canonical_set_matches_derived`), (c) close GOV-12 with L03 as the closer (3 loops · full detector-to-fix cycle demonstrated · GOV-13 substrate carries forward unauthored candidates).

Verify `reconciliation_signal: null` change since GOV-10 (was 72, now null on prod casey-junior /api/reconciliation/health) — API shape change vs. real degradation. Surfaced at GOV-12 L01 sweep; flagged carryforward, not GOV-shaped.

Git-commit hook scope-derivation refactor — sprint_code violations trending 133 (GOV-11 L02) → 135 (GOV-12 L01) → 135 (GOV-12 L02) → 136 (GOV-12 L03). Detector working; fix is the missing follow-up. Operator-route or future GOV scope.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-12 is OPEN with L01 + L02 + L03 closed. **Tonight 23:47** the `observatory-telemetry-contract-check.timer` fires the first automatic 12-contract run under cron; D-CFG-1 first-pair will report PASS post-L03. Next session: fire L04 (operator pick from backlog #3-8 in `governance-thread/docs/foreman/sprints/GOV-12/sprint-log.md`) OR close GOV-12 and let GOV-13 open from a fresh sweep. The L03 win — surgical detector-to-fix cycle in one loop — is a clean closer if you want to land the sprint with a complete pattern demo. Highest-leverage L04 candidates: (a) D-DOC-1 triage round 1 (operator-direct strike of `darntech-foreman/*` refs + template placeholders, ratchets 1060 baseline down meaningfully), (b) D-CFG-1 second-pair (Casey deployment-names ↔ darntech composables · builds dispatch generalization). Standing instruments unchanged.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + contracts in darntech telemetry-contracts.json (12 contracts as of GOV-12 L02)
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
work_locus: canonical darntech (scripts/telemetry-contract-check.sh + observatory/data/telemetry-contracts.json) — extending the GOV-11 contract framework
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-12 opened 2026-05-17 same-day after GOV-11 close. Substrate doc at governance-thread/docs/foreman/sprints/GOV-12/substrate.md was authored at GOV-11 close (operator picked option (a): close GOV-11, queue these as substrate). The 4 detector candidates (D-DOC-1, D-STALE-1/2/3, D-META-1, D-CFG-1) generalize patterns that GOV-11 L02's 5 contracts validated. L02 bundle (D-DOC-1 + D-CFG-1-first-pair) picked at L01 fire after pre-verifying D-CFG-1 drift; D-STALE-* and D-META-1 deferred (zero findings today / largest scope).
stakes: Same family as GOV-11 — extending the telemetry-contract framework to the next layer of [[canonical-source-per-fact]] coverage. GOV-11 caught drift at the data layer (EllaBot entries) + observatory artifacts. GOV-12 extends to the DOC layer (D-DOC-1: every path/wikilink in active docs must resolve) and the CONFIG layer (D-CFG-1: canonical sets and their derived configs must stay 1:1). If a doc cites a renamed file or a config lags a canonical taxonomy update, the dashboard tile reflects it within 24h. The whole pattern is: codified discipline → mechanical enforcement at the surface the discipline applies to.
notes: GOV-12 inherits all standing instruments unchanged (no new systemd units needed — both new contracts ride the existing observatory-telemetry-contract-check.timer @ 23:47 nightly). The pattern is now n=3 GOV sprints that ship contracts (GOV-11 shipped 5, GOV-12 shipped 2 with high tuning surface for D-DOC-1, future GOV-N will likely ship 1-4 each). Substrate doc at GOV-12/substrate.md still has 2 unauthored candidates (D-STALE trio + D-META-1) plus D-CFG-1 remaining 3 pairs — all are L03+ or GOV-13 candidates. Related memory: canonical-source-per-fact (the discipline both L02 contracts mechanize), fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation (same family at the action-surface layer), engine-identity-at-instance-health (same family at the runtime layer).

carryover_decisions_at_open.GOV-11_carryforward_drifts: 4 drifts surfaced at GOV-11 L02 first-fire remain operator-routing-queued (NULL project_slug 91/98 · 135 git-commit sprint_code violations · 2 true-orphan observatory files · dellatech bus cycle_label TBD). Each detector continues to surface its drift nightly — drift status is tracked in the dashboard tile, not in this manifest.
